data.title: 2025 National Christmas Tree Lighting features star-studded lineup including The Beach Boys, Gabby Barrett, Brett Young, and more. Airing tonight at 8 p.m. ET / 7 p.m. CT.
data.description: A highly-anticipated event showcasing a lineup of renowned artists such as The Beach Boys, Gabby Barrett, Brett Young, Jon Pardi, Warren Zeiders, Matthew West, Jillian Cardarelli, Alana Springsteen, and others will be broadcast tonight on December 5th at 8 p.m. ET / 7 p.m. CT. The exclusive airing will take place on Great American Family. This star-studded celebration is set to captivate audiences with their talent.

data.title: China Automotive Systems (NASDAQ: CAAS) progresses High-Torque Intelligent Steering Motors for Commercial Vehicles; subsidiary starts mass production in Wuhan, China.
data.description: In Wuhan, China on December 5, 2025, China Automotive Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: CAAS), a prominent provider of power steering components in China, revealed that its subsidiary, Hyoseong (Wuhan) Motion Mechatronics System Co. Ltd., has recently engaged in a new venture.

data.title: Cango Inc. reports November 2025 Bitcoin production and mining operations update on NYSE: CANG.
data.description: Cango Inc. (NYSE: CANG) released an update on its Bitcoin production and mining activities in November 2025. The report provided key metrics comparing November and October 2025 performance. The details include various statistics related to Bitcoin mining operations during the mentioned period. The information was shared without specifying the source.

data.title: Decoy introduces 80-calorie Cabernet Sauvignon in lower-calorie, lower-alcohol red wine category. #Wine #Decoy #NewYear
data.description: A popular premium wine brand has launched a new Cabernet Sauvignon in the lower-calorie and lower-alcohol red wine market, containing just 80 calories. This new product is introduced as the number one choice and arrives ahead of the New Year. The brand, headquartered in St. Helena, California, is known as Decoy and holds the top spot in the premium domestic wine market segment priced at $15 and above.

data.title: Immatics to sell 12.5 million shares at $10 each in an underwritten offering, expecting $125 million gross proceeds.
data.description: On December 5, 2025, Immatics N.V., a leading biopharmaceutical company based in Houston, Texas and Tuebingen, Germany, revealed plans for an underwritten offering. The offering involves the sale of 12,500,000 ordinary shares at a price of $10.00 per share, resulting in anticipated gross proceeds of $125 million. The completion of the offering is scheduled for December 8, 2025, pending the fulfillment of customary closing conditions. Immatics, recognized for its precision targeting of PRAME, operates on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol IMTX.

data.title: Organigram to report Q4 fiscal 2025 results on Dec 16, 2025. #1 cannabis company in Canada by market share. Conference call at 8am ET.
data.description: Organigram Global Inc., listed on NASDAQ (OGI) and TSX (OGI), Canada's top cannabis company by market share, is set to release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 earnings on Tuesday, December 16, 2025, before the market opens. A conference call to discuss the results will follow on the same day at 8:00 am Eastern Time. Investors and analysts can register for the call to obtain more details.

data.title: Yield Basis activates Fee Switch, distributing 17.55 BTC in fees to veYB holders. Milestone achieved by Michael Egorov's protocol.
data.description: In Zug, Switzerland, on December 5, 2025, Yield Basis, the liquidity protocol centered on Bitcoin and established by Michael Egorov, has achieved a significant milestone by activating the Fee Switch. This event marks the beginning of the distribution of captured fees.

data.title: Top press releases from Nov: Hershey & Dutch Bros holiday treats, Economist's 2026 predictions, Kimberly-Clark acquires Kenvue.
data.description: In New York on December 5, 2025, multiple notable events were reported. This included the introduction of holiday treats from Hershey and Dutch Bros, along with The Economist's forecast for the year 2026. Furthermore, Kimberly-Clark announced its purchase of Kenvue. These updates showcase the diverse range of developments in the business world, with specific emphasis on new products and strategic acquisitions. Stay updated on PR to not miss out on crucial information like this.

data.title: Microinsurance market valued at USD 73.76 billion in 2025, forecasted to reach USD 98.56 billion by 2030 at 6% CAGR.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, as of December 5, 2025, the microinsurance sector has been reported to be worth $73.76 billion. Analysts at Mordor Intelligence have projected that this market is set to increase to $98.56 billion by 2030, representing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 5.97%. The growth of this sector is primarily driven by the increasing adoption of mobile money services, among other factors.

data.title: Palantir partners with Northslope, their first elite AI partner, for expanded collaboration. Northslope founded in 2024 by Palantir alumni.
data.description: Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR) announced on Thursday an increased partnership with Northslope, an applied AI company founded in 2024 by Palantir alumni. Northslope is recognized as a top Palantir partner, specializing in creating AI applications for global clients. Together, they team up with major organizations to provide efficient AI solutions with high-level security measures. Under the agreement, Northslope is now the first member of Palantir's exclusive network. The collaboration aims to drive AI advancements and innovation on a global scale. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.
Japan stresses the need for stock, FX, and bond markets to trade in line with fundamentals, says Economy Minister Kiuchi.
Japan’s Economy Minister Kiuchi says he hopes the BOJ guides appropriate monetary policy to achieve the 2% inflation target, working closely with the government per their joint agreement.
Japan will keep a vigilant and urgent eye on market developments, says Economy Minister Kiuchi.
